intro:
Real architectural challenges I've solved — the problem, the decisions, and the technical blueprints.
No proprietary code shared; focusing on the engineering logic.

studies.label: CASE_001 · ERP / Industrial
studies.title: Server-Driven UI Framework with Custom DAO
studies.subtitle: Dannie.CC · 2023–2024
studies.problem: Manufacturing facility with 9 departments required real-time UI updates across complex production lines. Standard deployment cycles (3-4 hours) were blocking business velocity, and legacy database interactions were becoming untraceable.
approach: Architected a **TypeScript-based Platform** delivering JSON UI descriptors over WebSocket channels.
Designed and built **DAO (Data Access Object)**: a custom ArangoDB ORM for high-performance graph/document queries and audit-safe ledgering.
Implemented a **Dynamic Frontend Runtime** in React that renders complex terminal views from server-side fragment definitions.
Centralized all business logic in the backend, enabling daily UI iterations with zero client-side deployments.

decisions.title: WebSocket over HTTP
decisions.rationale: Eliminated 2s polling latency for quality control decisions.

decisions.title: Custom ORM (DAO)
decisions.rationale: Optimized complex manufacturing graph queries while ensuring strict data traceability.

studies.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant B as Backend (Node.js/TS)
    participant D as DAO (Custom ORM)
    participant A as ArangoDB
    participant W as WebSocket (SDUI Loop)
    participant F as Dynamic React Frontend

    F->>W: Initial Connection (Dept ID)
    W->>B: User Session Handshake
    B->>D: queryProductionGraph()
    D->>A: AQL Query execution
    A-->>D: Graph Result
    D-->>B: Entity Mapping
    B->>B: Generate UI Descriptor (JSON)
    B->>W: Push Module Definitions + Data
    W->>F: Render Components (Fragments)
studies.outcome: Digitized 9 departments with an 80% reduction in frontend overhead. The system became the backbone for IATF 16949 certification by ensuring 100% server-side logic auditability.

studies.label: CASE_002 · HealthTech / FHIR Compliance
studies.title: Professional IoT & FHIR Data Platform
studies.subtitle: Actimi GmbH · 2022–2023
studies.problem: Building a remote monitoring platform for German providers required strict FHIR R4 and GDPR compliance. Existing medical data storage lacked proper clinical resource indexing, and device lifecycle management was manually error-prone.
approach: Integrated **IoT Medical Devices** (Tablets, ECG, Blood Pressure monitors) into a unified clinical pipeline.
Automated **Device Lifecycle Management**: factory wipe + UUID regeneration via MDM for 100% GDPR traceability.
Redesigned the database with **PostgreSQL GIN Indexes** for high-frequency clinical FHIR searches (Patient, Observation, Encounter).
Implemented vendor-agnostic **FHIR Adapters** supporting both Aidbox and Medplum layers.

decisions.title: Dual-Vendor Adapter Layer
decisions.rationale: Avoided lock-in, allowing seamless switching between Aidbox and Medplum.

decisions.title: Hardware-Level Data Wipe
decisions.rationale: Ensured verifiable GDPR erasure that satisfied German DPA requirement.

studies.diagram:
graph LR
    subgraph Devices [IoT Devices]
        HW1[Tablet]
        HW2[ECG]
        HW3[BP]
    end
    Devices --> IGW[IoT Gateway]
    IGW --> |MQTT| FS[FHIR Server]
    FS --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
    DB --> CL[Dashboard]
    MDM[MDM Service] -.-> |GDPR Wipe| HW1
studies.outcome: Reduced clinical query latency from 800ms+ to 560ms while successfully passing three GDPR audits and scaling across Germany.

studies.label: CASE_003 · Fintech / High-Performance Crypto
studies.title: High-Frequency Crypto Trading & Copy-Trade Engine
studies.subtitle: DigiAlpha · 2021–2022
studies.problem: DigiAlpha needed a scalable exchange architecture for thousands of orders per second. The challenge was building a lock-free matching engine while managing complex multi-currency positions and virtual ledger balances.
approach: Designed a **Single-Threaded Matching Engine** avoiding distributed consensus latency, fed by ordered event logs.
Built a **Microservices Mesh** handling KYC, Payment (Fiat/Crypto), Order Management, and Wallet services.
Developed an **Internal Double-Entry Ledger** for instant virtual settlements, reconciled asynchronously with blockchain confirmations.
Implemented **Copy-Trading Logic** using message broadcasting via RabbitMQ to trigger mirror orders with near-zero latency.

decisions.title: Event-Driven Partitioning
decisions.rationale: Used RabbitMQ to broadcast price updates and wallet movements across services effortlessly.

decisions.title: Internal Virtual Ledger
decisions.rationale: Avoided blockchain confirmation bottlenecks for a fluent, real-time trading experience.

studies.diagram:
graph LR
    U[User Client] --> AG[API Gateway]
    AG --> KYC[KYC Service]
    AG --> OS[Order Service]
    OS --> RMQ{RabbitMQ ordered log}
    RMQ --> ME[Matching Engine]
    ME --> L[Internal Ledger]
    ME --> WS[Wallet Service]
    WS --> HDW[HD Wallet - Master Seed]
    L --> TR[Trade Reconciliation]
    ME -.-> |Broadcast| CT[Copy-Trade Engine]
studies.outcome: Launched 5 trading modes in 12 months with zero data loss. The modular architecture easily scaled to support high-volume Copy-Trading features.
